model_narrative: We are planning a closed route that visits a collection of cities. For every ordered pair of cities we decide whether the route travels directly from the first to the second. Each direct leg between two cities has a known travel cost, and the goal is to choose the legs so that the total travel cost of the route is as small as possible.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the cities, treated as origins; each city is a place the tour passes through

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the cities, treated as destinations; this is the same collection of cities as the origin set, used to name where an arc lands

params.name: c
params.doc: the cost of travelling directly from the origin city to the destination city, in distance units; the cost from a city to itself is zero

vars.name: x
vars.doc: a binary indicator that is one when the route goes directly from the origin city to the destination city and zero otherwise; the entries from a city to itself are held at zero so no city links to itself

constraints.name: x[i,
constraints.intent: The route must depart from every city exactly once. For each origin city, exactly one direct leg leaving that city to some destination city is chosen.

constraints.name: x[i,
constraints.intent: The route must arrive at every city exactly once. For each destination city, exactly one direct leg coming into that city from some origin city is chosen.

query: We're looking at a routing rule where city 1's outgoing link has to land on one of the two far depots, city 6 or city 7. Require city 1 to send its link to at least one of those two and tell me what the assignment total becomes.

query: Suppose the link between city 1 and city 5 is unavailable in both directions. Drop that connection and let me see the new assignment cost.

query: There's a proposal on the table to stop cities 2, 3 and 4 from staying sealed off together. We'd require that whole group to send all of its outgoing links to the rest of the network — none kept inside the trio. Apply that and tell me what the assignment ends up costing.
expected_pyomo: model.cross_234 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x[a, b] for a in ['i2', 'i3', 'i4'] for b in ['i1', 'i5', 'i6', 'i7']) >= 3)

query: Cities 1 and 5 just ping-pong straight off each other in this plan. Couldn't we stop them forming that closed pair and route through them as part of a longer chain? What's pulling them into a two-city loop?
